MAPLE WINDS HEALTHCARE AND REHABILITATION, LLC

CCN: 396088 · PORTAGE, PA 15946 · Cambria County

Overview

Address
4112 SPRING HILL ROAD, PORTAGE, PA 15946
Phone
8147366006
Certified beds
50
Avg daily residents
43 (86% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2003-09-11
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.39 3.73 4.54 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.04 0.65 1.08 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.83 0.83 0.85 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.52 2.25 2.60 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.87 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.91
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.81
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.05
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
61.5%
annual
RN turnover
30.0%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.32
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-02 17 17 3 96 1 96
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-06-05 24 15 9 128 2 192
Total weighted health score
120.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (51)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. E 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. E 2025-07-02 2025-08-12
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-12-26 2025-02-04
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-12-26 2025-02-04
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-12-26 2025-02-04
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. E 2024-12-26 2025-02-04
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-07-11 2024-07-30
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-06-05 2024-08-20
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. D 2024-06-05 2024-07-16
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2024-01-26 2024-03-04
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2024-01-26 2024-03-04
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-01-26 2024-03-04
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. E 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-06-01 2023-07-10
